search_text: EfficientNeRF: Efficient Neural Radiance Fields EfficientNeRF：高效神经辐射场 Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) has been wildly applied to various tasks for its high-quality representation of 3D scenes. It takes long per-scene training time and per-image testing time. In this paper, we present EfficientNeRF as an efficient NeRF-based method to represent 3D scene and synthesize novel-view images. Although several ways exist to accelerate the training or testing process, it is still difficult to much reduce time for both phases simultaneously. We analyze the density and weight distribution of the sampled points then propose valid and pivotal sampling at the coarse and fine stage, respectively, to significantly improve sampling efficiency. In addition, we design a novel data structure to cache the whole scene during testing to accelerate the rendering speed. Overall, our method can reduce over 88\% of training time, reach rendering speed of over 200 FPS, while still achieving competitive accuracy. Experiments prove that our method promotes the practicality of NeRF in the real world and enables many applications. 神经辐射场 (NeRF) 因其高质量的 3D 场景表示而被广泛应用于各种任务。每个场景的训练时间和每个图像的测试时间都较长。在本文中，我们提出 EfficientNeRF 作为一种高效的基于 NeRF 的方法来表示 3D 场景并合成新颖的视图图像。尽管有多种方法可以加速训练或测试过程，但同时大幅减少两个阶段的时间仍然很困难。我们分析采样点的密度和权重分布，然后分别在粗略和精细阶段提出有效采样和关键采样，以显着提高采样效率。此外，我们设计了一种新颖的数据结构来在测试过程中缓存整个场景，以加快渲染速度。总的来说，我们的方法可以减少超过 88% 的训练时间，达到超过 200 FPS 的渲染速度，同时仍然达到有竞争力的准确性。实验证明我们的方法促进了 NeRF 在现实世界中的实用性并实现了许多应用。 本文提出了一种名为EfficientNeRF的高效神经辐射场方法，旨在减少NeRF在训练和测试阶段的时间消耗。 Tao Hu,Shu Liu,Yilun Chen,Tiancheng Shen,Jiaya Jia cs.CV

authors_text: Haoran You,Cheng Wan,Yang Zhao,Zhongzhi Yu,Yonggan Fu,Jiayi Yuan,Shang Wu,Shunyao Zhang,Yongan Zhang,Chaojian Li,Vivek Boominathan,Ashok Veeraraghavan,Ziyun Li,Yingyan Celine Lin
title: EyeCoD: Eye Tracking System Acceleration via FlatCam-based Algorithm & Accelerator Co-Design
title_zh: EyeCoD：通过基于 FlatCam 的算法和加速器协同设计加速眼动追踪系统
comment: Accepted by ISCA 2022; Also selected as an IEEE Micro's Top Pick of 2023
comments: Accepted by ISCA 2022; Also selected as an IEEE Micro's Top Pick of 2023
abstract: Eye tracking has become an essential human-machine interaction modality for providing immersive experience in numerous virtual and augmented reality (VR/AR) applications desiring high throughput (e.g., 240 FPS), small-form, and enhanced visual privacy. However, existing eye tracking systems are still limited by their: (1) large form-factor largely due to the adopted bulky lens-based cameras; and (2) high communication cost required between the camera and backend processor, thus prohibiting their more extensive applications. To this end, we propose a lensless FlatCam-based eye tracking algorithm and accelerator co-design framework dubbed EyeCoD to enable eye tracking systems with a much reduced form-factor and boosted system efficiency without sacrificing the tracking accuracy, paving the way for next-generation eye tracking solutions. On the system level, we advocate the use of lensless FlatCams to facilitate the small form-factor need in mobile eye tracking systems. On the algorithm level, EyeCoD integrates a predict-then-focus pipeline that first predicts the region-of-interest (ROI) via segmentation and then only focuses on the ROI parts to estimate gaze directions, greatly reducing redundant computations and data movements. On the hardware level, we further develop a dedicated accelerator that (1) integrates a novel workload orchestration between the aforementioned segmentation and gaze estimation models, (2) leverages intra-channel reuse opportunities for depth-wise layers, and (3) utilizes input feature-wise partition to save activation memory size. On-silicon measurement validates that our EyeCoD consistently reduces both the communication and computation costs, leading to an overall system speedup of 10.95x, 3.21x, and 12.85x over CPUs, GPUs, and a prior-art eye tracking processor called CIS-GEP, respectively, while maintaining the tracking accuracy.
summary: Eye tracking has become an essential human-machine interaction modality for providing immersive experience in numerous virtual and augmented reality (VR/AR) applications desiring high throughput (e.g., 240 FPS), small-form, and enhanced visual privacy. However, existing eye tracking systems are still limited by their: (1) large form-factor largely due to the adopted bulky lens-based cameras; and (2) high communication cost required between the camera and backend processor, thus prohibiting their more extensive applications. To this end, we propose a lensless FlatCam-based eye tracking algorithm and accelerator co-design framework dubbed EyeCoD to enable eye tracking systems with a much reduced form-factor and boosted system efficiency without sacrificing the tracking accuracy, paving the way for next-generation eye tracking solutions. On the system level, we advocate the use of lensless FlatCams to facilitate the small form-factor need in mobile eye tracking systems. On the algorithm level, EyeCoD integrates a predict-then-focus pipeline that first predicts the region-of-interest (ROI) via segmentation and then only focuses on the ROI parts to estimate gaze directions, greatly reducing redundant computations and data movements. On the hardware level, we further develop a dedicated accelerator that (1) integrates a novel workload orchestration between the aforementioned segmentation and gaze estimation models, (2) leverages intra-channel reuse opportunities for depth-wise layers, and (3) utilizes input feature-wise partition to save activation memory size. On-silicon measurement validates that our EyeCoD consistently reduces both the communication and computation costs, leading to an overall system speedup of 10.95x, 3.21x, and 12.85x over CPUs, GPUs, and a prior-art eye tracking processor called CIS-GEP, respectively, while maintaining the tracking accuracy.
abstract_zh: 眼动追踪已成为一种重要的人机交互方式，可在众多需要高吞吐量（例如 240 FPS）、小型和增强视觉隐私的虚拟和增强现实 (VR/AR) 应用中提供沉浸式体验。然而，现有的眼动追踪系统仍然受到以下限制：（1）外形尺寸较大，这主要是由于采用了笨重的基于镜头的相机； (2)相机和后端处理器之间的通信成本较高，从而阻碍了其更广泛的应用。为此，我们提出了一种基于无透镜 FlatCam 的眼动追踪算法和名为 EyeCoD 的加速器协同设计框架，使眼动追踪系统能够在不牺牲跟踪精度的情况下大幅缩小外形尺寸并提高系统效率，从而为下一代眼动追踪解决方案铺平道路。在系统层面，我们提倡使用无透镜 FlatCam 来满足移动眼动追踪系统的小尺寸需求。在算法层面，EyeCoD 集成了预测然后聚焦的流程，首先通过分割预测感兴趣区域（ROI），然后仅关注 ROI 部分来估计注视方向，大大减少了冗余计算和数据移动。在硬件层面，我们进一步开发了一个专用加速器，它（1）在上述分割和注视估计模型之间集成了一种新颖的工作负载编排，（2）利用深度层的通道内重用机会，以及（3）利用输入特征分区来节省激活内存大小。硅上测量验证了我们的 EyeCoD 持续降低了通信和计算成本，与 CPU、GPU 和称为 CIS-GEP 的现有眼动追踪处理器相比，整体系统速度分别提高了 10.95 倍、3.21 倍和 12.85 倍，同时保持了跟踪精度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出EyeCoD系统，通过FlatCam镜头-less算法与加速器协同设计，实现小型化、高效的眼动追踪。
AI.motivation: 现有眼动追踪系统因使用传统镜头相机导致体积大、通信成本高，难以满足VR/AR应用对小型化、高吞吐量和隐私保护的需求。
AI.method: 采用镜头-less FlatCam实现小型化；算法层面通过分割预测ROI并聚焦计算降低冗余；硬件层面设计专用加速器优化模型协同、通道复用与内存分区。
AI.result: 系统通信与计算成本显著降低，相比CPU、GPU及CIS-GEP处理器分别提速10.95x、3.21x和12.85x，且保持追踪精度。
AI.conclusion: EyeCoD通过算法-硬件协同设计有效解决了眼动追踪系统的体积与效率瓶颈，为下一代解决方案奠定基础。
